title: Canaria tortuosa
intro: Canaria tortuosa, also known as Canarium commune or Canarium indicum, is a tropical tree species native to the tropical forests of the Indo-Pacific region, belonging to the family Burseraceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of various conditions, including fever, pain, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Canaria tortuosa include canariumin, canarium oil, and various terpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds that contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Canaria tortuosa include essential oils, extracts, and traditional remedies such as decoctions and infusions, which are used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
taxonomy: Canaria tortuosa, also known as the twisted canary or crested date palm, is classified under the family Arecaceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, such as its palmate leaves and woody stem. More specifically, Canaria tortuosa is a species within the genus Phoenix. The species Canaria tortuosa is a synonym for the Phoenix canariensis which is native to the Canary Islands. It is often confused with the Phoenix dactylifera due to its similarity in appearance.
